topic: Area of Study 7: Art Music since 1910
subject: music

items.correct: A recurring main theme (A) alternates with contrasting episodes (B, C, etc.).
items.misconception: A round where voices enter at different times.
items.why: Students confuse rondo (alternating refrain) with round (canon). Rondo: ABACA, ABACABA, etc. The A theme keeps coming back, like a refrain.

items.q: What does modulation mean in music?
items.correct: Changing from one key to another within a piece.
items.why: Students conflate modulation with any musical change. Specifically, modulation is a KEY CHANGE, usually established by a perfect cadence in the new key - a structural device.

items.correct: Two or more independent melodic lines sounding together.
items.misconception: A single melody with chord accompaniment.
items.why: Students confuse polyphony with thick texture or homophony. Polyphony specifically means INDEPENDENT MELODIES - like a fugue. Melody + chords is HOMOPHONY, not polyphony.
It's Schoenberg. “Stravinsky” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which composer is most associated with minimalist works such as 'Music for 18 Musicians'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Reich. “Glass” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did John Cage compose his silent work '4'33"'?
items.why: The real figure is 1952. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
